column1: # DSFB Oil & Gas — Historian / SCADA Generic Tag Template
# This file documents the minimum tag schema required for any generic historian
# or SCADA integration with the DSFB residual structuring engine.
# Column conventions:
#   timestamp          : Unix epoch seconds (float64) or ISO-8601 string.
#                        Required. Must be monotonically non-decreasing.
#   expected_<signal>  : Model or setpoint value for the channel (float64).
#                        Source: RTTM
#   observed_<signal>  : Live or historian-archived measurement (float64).
#                        Source: DCS historian tag or OSIsoft PI tag.
#   <auxiliary_1..N>   : Optional contextual columns (e.g.
#                        Not used by the residual engine but logged for context.
# Notes:
#   - All values must be numeric after type conversion.  Text
#     and engineering-unit labels must be stripped before ingestion.
#   - Missing values (NaN
column2: empty string) will cause a ParseFloat error.

column1: #     Impute or filter upstream before passing to the DSFB loader.
#   - The DSFB engine is read-only: it writes no tags back to the historian.
# LIMITATIONS:
#   The DSFB framework provides NO quality assessment of sensor readings
#   NO calibration drift detection
#   Garbage-in produces structured garbage-out.
timestamp
